items.description: Faint irregular gray smudge with soft diffuse edges.
items.scholarly_note: Age-related discoloration or light handling stain.

items.description: Prominent dark gray irregular blotch with darker core and mottled edges, approx. 4-5 cm across.
items.scholarly_note: Possible water stain, dirt accumulation, or ink transfer.

items.description: Scattered tiny dark particulate specks and light general foxing.
items.scholarly_note: Typical aging and environmental soiling for a medieval manuscript leaf.

items.description: Three small circular holes aligned vertically near the gutter edge, each roughly 1-2 mm in diameter.
Rounded and abraded corner with minor material loss and fraying.
Small notch or chip on the upper right edge.
Multiple faint hairline scratches and fine surface abrasions appearing as thin linear marks.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly from quill scraping, parchment preparation, or later handling.

scratches: Faint hairline linear abrasions in lower half of leaf
Subtle thin surface scoring visible across multiple areas
damage_notes: Three circular binding/sewing holes in right margin
Minor loss and rounding at bottom right corner
Small notch on top right edge
Slightly irregular and chipped right edge
summary: Folio appears blank with no visible text, marginalia, doodles, pen trials, or corrections. Only physical condition features are present: scattered faint to moderate stains, binding holes, edge wear, corner loss, and faint surface scratches consistent with age and handling of a medieval manuscript.
